entries.label: #[derive(...)]
entries.note: Auto-implement traits; most common attribute in Rust

entries.label: Copy + Clone
entries.code:
#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
struct Point { x: f64, y: f64 }
entries.note: Copy requires Clone; all fields must be Copy

entries.label: Ordering derives
entries.note: Eq requires PartialEq; Ord requires PartialOrd + Eq

entries.label: Serde derives
entries.code:
#[derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
struct ApiResponse {
    status_code: u32,
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    error_message: Option<String>,
}
entries.note: Popular third-party derive for serialization/deserialization

entries.label: #[test]
entries.note: Marks a function as a unit test; run with cargo test

entries.label: #[should_panic]
entries.note: Test passes if it panics with the expected message

entries.label: #[ignore]
entries.note: Skip by default; run explicitly with --ignored flag

entries.label: #[cfg(test)]
entries.note: Module only compiled when running tests; convention for unit tests

entries.label: #[cfg(...)]
entries.note: Include item only when condition is true; checked at compile time

entries.label: #[cfg(feature)]
entries.note: Gate code on Cargo features; combine with not() for fallback

entries.label: cfg combinations
entries.note: all() = AND, any() = OR, not() = NOT; can be nested

entries.label: #[cfg_attr]
entries.note: Conditionally apply another attribute based on cfg condition

entries.label: cfg! macro
entries.note: Runtime-accessible cfg check; unlike #[cfg], both branches must compile

entries.label: #[inline]
entries.note: Hint to compiler; inline(always) forces inlining; inline(never) prevents it

entries.label: #[must_use]
entries.note: Compiler warns if return value is unused; great for Result and guard types

entries.label: #[repr(...)]
entries.note: Control memory layout; C for FFI; u8/u32 for enum discriminant; transparent for single-field

entries.label: #[non_exhaustive]
entries.note: Prevent external code from exhaustively matching or constructing; allows adding variants/fields later

entries.label: #[cold]
entries.note: Marks function as unlikely to be called; helps optimizer with branch prediction

entries.label: #[allow(...)]
entries.note: Suppress specific compiler warnings locally

entries.label: #[warn(...)]
entries.note: Enable a warning that may be off by default

entries.label: #[deny(...)]
entries.note: Turn a warning into a hard error

entries.label: #[forbid(...)]
entries.code:
#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
// Cannot be overridden with #[allow] in this crate
entries.note: Like deny but cannot be overridden by allow; strictest level

entries.label: Common lint names
entries.note: Full list: rustc -W help or clippy lint list online

entries.label: Inner attributes (#![...])
entries.note: Apply to the enclosing item (crate or module); placed at top of file

entries.label: Feature gates
entries.code:
#![feature(let_chains)]
#![feature(generic_const_exprs)]
entries.note: Enable unstable features; requires nightly compiler

entries.label: Crate metadata
entries.note: Usually set in Cargo.toml instead; doc attribute for crate-level docs

entries.label: Windows subsystem
entries.code: #![windows_subsystem = "windows"]
entries.note: Prevents console window from appearing for GUI apps on Windows

entries.label: Doc comments as attributes
entries.note: /// is syntactic sugar for #[doc = "..."]

entries.label: Doc hidden
entries.code:
#[doc(hidden)]
pub fn internal_api() { }
entries.note: Public but hidden from documentation; for internal use

entries.label: Doc aliases
entries.note: Allow searching docs by alternative names

entries.label: Module-level docs
entries.note: //! documents the containing item; placed at top of file/module

entries.label: #[tokio::main]
entries.note: Sets up the Tokio runtime; transforms main into a synchronous function internally

entries.label: #[tokio::test]
entries.note: Async test runner; sets up runtime for each test

entries.label: #[global_allocator]
entries.note: Set the global memory allocator; default is usually jemalloc or system

entries.label: #[panic_handler]
entries.note: Required in no_std binaries; defines what happens on panic
